However, `trace(A / sqrt(n)) = (∑_i A_{i,i}) / sqrt(n) = (∑_i X_{i,i}) / sqrt(n)` because for the diagonal, we have `i = j`, and `A_{i,i} = X_{i,i}`. So `E[trace(A / sqrt(n))] = (n E[X_{1,1}])/sqrt(n) = sqrt(n) E[X_{1,1}]`, but then `(1 / n) E[trace(A / sqrt(n))] = E[X_{1,1}] / sqrt(n) → 0` as `n → ∞`.

So then `wignerMatrixScaledTracePowerSequence μ ν 1 = (1 / n) * sqrt(n) E[X_{1,1}] = E[X_{1,1}] / sqrt(n) → 0` as `n → ∞`.
